title: Sales Qualified Lead
shortDefinition: A prospect vetted by sales as ready for engagement and likely to convert into a customer.

content.definition: A Sales Qualified Lead (SQL) is an MQL that has been reviewed and approved by your sales team as worth their time. They meet your target customer criteria and show clear buying intent—they're actively evaluating solutions, have budget, and are in an actual decision-making process. SQLs are the handoff point where marketing stops and sales takes over.
content.whyItMatters: This is where marketing and sales alignment actually matters. When your sales team trusts the quality of SQLs coming from marketing, everybody wins: sales closes faster, marketing gets better feedback on which lead sources work, and you stop wasting resources on prospects who were never going to buy. For bootstrapped teams, this focus prevents the sales team from chasing dead ends while legitimate buyers slip away.
content.howToApply: Define your SQL criteria in collaboration with your sales team. Typical signals: active tool evaluation, pricing page visits, demo request, competitor research, budget and timeline indicators. Have sales explicitly qualify or disqualify each MQL, then track why they rejected leads—this feedback loops back to improve MQL criteria. Measure the conversion rate from SQL to customer (your close rate). Focus on increasing the quality of SQLs (higher close rate) even if it means fewer SQLs. IdeaFuel's Business Plan Generator helps you establish SQL criteria and build a consistent sales handoff process that improves team alignment.
commonMistakes: Sales and marketing never defining SQL criteria—this creates endless conflict about lead quality. Lock this definition down together.
Counting demos given as SQLs—a demo request is potential SQL signal, but not guaranteed. Qualify intent before calling it SQL.
Not tracking SQL-to-customer conversion—if 10% of SQLs become customers, you can mathematize the business. Without this metric, you're flying blind.
content.ideafuelConnection: IdeaFuel's Business Plan Generator helps you define SQL criteria and build a consistent sales handoff process that aligns marketing and sales around qualified prospects.

seoTitle: What is a Sales Qualified Lead? SQL Definition & Criteria | IdeaFuel Glossary
seoDescription: SQL is a prospect qualified for sales engagement with clear buying intent. Learn how to identify and manage SQLs.
